source.title: The Reorienting System of the Human Brain: From Environment to Theory of Mind

confidence_rationale: Corbetta et al. introduce the 'circuit breaker' metaphor explicitly: the ventral attention network (which includes anterior insula) interrupts ongoing dorsal-network selection when a salient, behaviourally relevant event is detected. This is the most explicit biological statement of a discrete interrupt mechanism analogous to MECH-259's mode-switch. However, the 2008 paper says the ventral network 'facilitates rather than initiates' reconfiguration and describes transient rather than sustained mode changes, softening the discrete-switch characterisation. Mapping fidelity is moderate because the paper's primary domain (exogenous attention capture) is narrower than MECH-259's full operating_mode switching across internal/external task modes.
failure_signatures: The ventral network is characterised as facilitating reconfiguration rather than being the sole or direct initiator, leaving open whether a purely graded implementation would be functionally equivalent.
The 'transient activation' characterisation of the ventral network is consistent with a discrete event-triggered signal but does not rule out a steeply graded one.
The interrupt model applies specifically to exogenous attention capture; extension to full operating_mode transitions (e.g. external_task -> internal_planning) is an extrapolation.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The right-hemisphere ventral frontoparietal network (including anterior insula) acts as a 'circuit breaker' that interrupts ongoing dorsal-network selection when a salient, unexpected, or task-relevant stimulus is detected; the interruption resets the system to allow reorienting.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-259 proposes that when salience aggregate exceeds a threshold, SD-032a fires a discrete operating_mode switch rather than a smooth policy update. Corbetta et al.'s circuit-breaker model is the closest biological analogue: the ventral network (AI-anchored) produces an event-triggered interrupt, not a continuous modulation signal. The 'reset' characterisation maps onto the Schmitt-trigger hysteresis in the implementation -- once the switch fires, it commits to the new mode rather than immediately reverting.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Corbetta circuit-breaker is about attentional reorienting (exogenous capture of dorsal-network task focus); MECH-259 is about a broader operating_mode coordinator managing external_task / internal_planning / safe_exploration modes. The interrupt mechanism is analogous but the target system and state space differ. Transfer risk is moderate.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI; visual and auditory target detection tasks; attention reorienting paradigms; healthy adults.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Corbetta lab, Neuron, extensively replicated). Mapping fidelity moderate -- the interrupt metaphor is directly relevant but the domain (attentional reorienting) is narrower than MECH-259's full mode-management scope. Transfer risk reflects the narrower domain.
